label: 2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
race_name: Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres

extraction.one_line_summary: ASGREEN attacked on Oude Kwaremont with ALAPHILIPPE, dropped VAN AERT on Paterberg, then outsprinted VAN DER POEL after the Dutchman fatally decelerated in final 50 meters.
tactical_patterns: ASGREEN bridged to ALAPHILIPPE's move at 39km with VAN AERT and VAN DER POEL, proving E3 winners carry form.
VAN DER POEL attacked early to isolate VAN AERT before crucial climbs, reversing 2020 patience tactics that won him the race.
ALAPHILIPPE's Oude Kwaremont acceleration gave teammate ASGREEN the bridge opportunity, decoupage world champion sat in chasing group after.
VAN BAARLE led quality chase group that absorbed VAN AERT after Paterberg, revealing INEOS committed riders behind front trio.

rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: converted E3 form into Ronde victory by reading ALAPHILIPPE's attack perfectly and holding superior sprint power deep
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: tactical aggression at 39km correct but unprecedented sprint deceleration in final 50m suggests power collapse
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: isolated by VAN DER POEL's early forcing, dropped on Paterberg despite making front group on Oude Kwaremont
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: world champion's attack created winning move for teammate but sat up rather than contesting finish himself
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: only Belgian podium finisher showed endurance chase ability absent from favorites VAN AERT and STUYVEN
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: led organized INEOS chase on Paterberg that reabsorbed VAN AERT, proving super-domestique quality in classics
rider_signals.TURGIS Anthony: positive: bridged to favorites group solo for Total Direct Energie before Paterberg selection, unexpected climbing strength
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: neutral: initiated early break and lasted longest from original seven before favorites' bridge on climbs
rider_signals.HAUSSLER Heinrich: neutral: attacked at 39km for Bahrain as favorites marked each other, showing team willing to gamble on counter-moves
